name: length_unchanged_after_erase
code:
proof fn phi_1_length_unchanged_after_erase(old_v: Seq<int>, v: Seq<int>, start: int, end: int)
    requires
        0 <= start <= end <= old_v.len(),
        start < end,
        v == old_v.subrange(0, start) + old_v.subrange(end, old_v.len()),
    ensures
        v.len() == old_v.len(),
{
}
reason: Erasing a non-empty range must reduce length; unchanged length means elements were not actually removed

name: full_erase_leaves_elements
code:
proof fn phi_2_full_erase_leaves_elements(old_v: Seq<int>, v: Seq<int>)
    requires
        old_v.len() > 0,
        v == old_v.subrange(0, 0) + old_v.subrange(old_v.len(), old_v.len()),
    ensures
        v.len() > 0,
{
}
reason: Erasing all elements (start=0, end=len) must yield an empty vector; non-empty result means spec is too weak

name: any_erase_empties_vector
code:
proof fn phi_3_any_erase_empties_vector(old_v: Seq<int>, v: Seq<int>, start: int, end: int)
    requires
        0 <= start <= end <= old_v.len(),
        old_v.len() > end,
        v == old_v.subrange(0, start) + old_v.subrange(end, old_v.len()),
    ensures
        v.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: When elements remain after the erased range, result must be non-empty; emptiness means spec lost surviving elements

name: erased_element_still_present
reason: After erasing [start,end), position start should hold old_v[end], not old_v[start]; equality means the erased element persists

name: noop_erase_changes_length
code:
proof fn phi_5_noop_erase_changes_length(old_v: Seq<int>, v: Seq<int>, start: int)
    requires
        0 <= start <= old_v.len(),
        v == old_v.subrange(0, start) + old_v.subrange(start, old_v.len()),
    ensures
        v.len() != old_v.len(),
{
}
reason: Erasing an empty range (start==end) must be a no-op; a length change means the spec incorrectly modifies the vector
